name: Omani Arabic

allophone_rules.notes: Velar affrication /k/ вЖТ [t Г] adjacent to a high front vowel /i, iЋР/. Al-Balushi 2016 (Macrolinguistics 4(1):80-125), p.89: 'while this affrication (from /k/ to /ƒН/) occurs in the Bedouin dialects in the vicinity of front vowels, it is unconditional in the sedentary dialects (Holes, 2013)' вАФ with examples bƒБƒНir 'tomorrow' (bƒБkir), simaƒН 'fish' (samak), ƒНiswah 'bride's clothes' (kiswah) from the Al-Buraimi, бєҐ≈Ђr and Ja ХlƒБn Bedouin dialects. Note that 'sedentary' is not uniform in Al-Balushi's account: the northern sedentary varieties of WƒБdi SaƒІtan and Al-ƒ¶amrƒБ DO affricate (yƒН≈Ђn,  ФaƒНil, ƒНƒУƒНah), and there the process is 'unconditional' (not restricted to the front-vowel environment), whereas the prestige sedentary koine of Muscat and бєҐalƒБlah does NOT productively affricate /k/, and Dhofari does not. LIMITATIONS: this rule models the front-vowel-conditioned Bedouin/interior pattern only; it therefore over-generalises toward the affricating varieties and cannot capture either the non-affricating Muscat/бєҐalƒБlah norm or the coda / unconditional-northern-sedentary distributions. UNAFFECTED by this rule: Old Arabic *q, which in Omani is retained as [q] (sedentary; grapheme ўВ lists [q] first), unlike the mainstream Gulf *qвЖТ[…°].

ancestors.notes: Peninsular Arabic base вАФ conservative consonant system, interdental retention, emphatic spreading (AR_PEN_EMPH_*).
Persian adstrate from Hormuz Strait trade and Muscat contacts.

notes: Omani Arabic вАФ a conservative Peninsular variety of the southeastern coast, genetically Peninsular (inherits ar-x-peninsular DIRECTLY, not the Gulf node). DISTINCTIVE from the northern Gulf: Old Arabic *q is RETAINED as [q] in the sedentary varieties of Muscat, бєҐalƒБlah and parts of the BƒБбє≠ina (Al-Balushi 2016 p.88: '...pronounce /q/ as /q/, as in ƒІaqq≈Ђti "my/mine"...'), while Bedouin varieties have *q вЖТ […°] (y-…°≈Ђl 'he says') and some northern-mountain sedentary varieties have *q вЖТ [k]; Sur/бєҐoƒІƒБr/бєҐaƒІam show *q вЖТ [d Т] (Holes 2014). The grapheme ўВ therefore lists [q] first (sedentary default), […°] second. jiim /d Т/ is realised as [d Т] in the (mainly Bedouin/northern) dialects that keep it and as […°] elsewhere (Glover 1988:38, cited Al-Balushi p.89: 'the voiced palatal affricate /«∞/ is a free variant of /g/ in most words'). Interdentals /ќЄ √∞ √∞Ћ§/ and emphatics are retained (Al-Balushi p.87). /k/вЖТ[t Г] affrication is present as a Bedouin/interior feature (allophone_rule OM_K_AFFRICATION; see its notes for the sedentary caveat). South Arabian (Mehri/Jibbali) adstrate in Dhofar; Swahili adstrate from the Omani-Zanzibar maritime empire; Persian adstrate from Hormuz trade. Input contract: fully diacritised (tashkeel) Arabic.

sources.author: Al-Balushi, R.
sources.title: Omani Arabic: More than a Dialect
sources.notes: Read the phonology sections (pp. 85-90, open-access PDF). Primary source for Omani /q/ reflexes (Bedouin […°]; sedentary Muscat/бєҐalƒБlah [q]; northern-mountain [k]; Sur/бєҐoƒІƒБr [d Т]) at p.88; jiim /d Т/~[…°] free variation (Glover 1988:38) at p.89; /k/вЖТ[t Г] Bedouin 'in the vicinity of front vowels' vs unconditional sedentary (Holes 2013) at p.89; interdental/emphatic retention at p.87; emphatic backing /a/вЖТ[…С] (mara√∞ћ£ [m…СбєЫ…С√∞Ћ§]) at p.90.

sources.author: Alshammari, K. K.
sources.title: An Optimality-Theoretic Account of Velar Affrication in Northern Najdi Arabic
sources.publisher: Journal of Language Teaching and Research 17(4)
sources.notes: Read in full (open-access PDF). Cross-reference for the Peninsular velar-affrication typology (front-vowel conditioning; historical/lexicalised status per Johnstone 1967) underlying OM_K_AFFRICATION.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Omani Arabic.

stress.notes: Arabic stress is QUANTITY-SENSITIVE: it falls on a syllable because that syllable is heavy, not because of any orthographic ending, so no ending table can express it. The cascade (Ryding, A Reference Grammar of MSA, CUP 2005, ¬І2.3; Watson, The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic, OUP 2002, ch.3): a superheavy final syllable (CVЋРC/CVCC) takes the stress (kiЋИtaЋРb); otherwise a heavy penult (CVЋР/CVC) does (muЋИdarris); otherwise the antepenult (ЋИmadrasa). max_onset=1 because Arabic onsets are obligatory and hold exactly one consonant вАФ a medial cluster splits, closing the preceding syllable, and that boundary is what makes the penult of mudarris heavy. INPUT CONTRACT: weight is only recoverable from a complete transcription, so this requires diacritized input; an undiacritized skeleton has no vowels to weigh. The registered wikipron gold carries no stress marks, but the benchmark strips stress from both sides by default, so this costs no PER.
